reasoning: Currently unable to browse for real-time chart data due to tool limitations, but based on historical Bruegel data, total EU imports from Russia (LNG + pipeline) have been hovering below 15 bcm per quarter since late 2022. With the cessation of Ukraine transit in Jan 2025, Q1 2025 imports are likely substantially constrained. TurkStream and LNG alone are highly unlikely to sum to 15 bcm in a single quarter. To reach 15 bcm per quarter, Russia would need to supply about 60 bcm/year. Given that in 2023 total imports from Russia were ~43 bcm (~10-11 bcm/quarter), and the Ukraine transit (~14-15 bcm/year) is now halted, the physical capacity and current trajectory severely limit the chance of reaching 15 bcm in any 2025 quarter. Thus, the probability is quite low.
